name: Aging blood commonly accrues predictable mosaic chromosomal alterations
summary: The latest UK Biobank whole-genome sequencing analysis strengthens the human biomarker landscape for mosaic chromosomal alterations in aging blood.
statement: Whole-genome sequencing of UK Biobank blood DNA found that mosaic chromosomal alterations are commonly detectable in older individuals and often accrue in predictable ways.
population_or_model: 484,081 UK Biobank participants without blood cancer diagnoses at assessment
quantitative_note: The analysis ascertained 43,617 autosomal mCAs; CLL-associated 13q14 deletions were detectable in 1% of individuals aged 65 to 70 years.
caveats: This is an observational biomarker and clonal-evolution map, not an intervention study.
The primary tissue is blood, so it does not measure chromosome stability across all aging tissues.
The study supports human relevance of the biomarker but does not identify a proven preventive treatment.
